title: Decision Support And Strategy
summary: Use AI to structure choices, stress-test reasoning, and communicate decisions with more clarity.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use AI As A Thinking Partner
lessons.subtitle: The model can improve decision quality by structuring options and tradeoffs.
lessons.objective: Use AI to support strategic thinking without handing over the decision itself.
lessons.useWhen: The model can improve decision quality by structuring options and tradeoffs.
lessons.whyItMatters: AI is helpful when the task is exploring options, assumptions, and implications. It is not the accountable decision-maker.

coreConcepts.title: Frame The Decision
coreConcepts.text: State what choice is being made, by whom, by when, and under which constraints.

coreConcepts.title: Ask For Structure
coreConcepts.text: Request options, assumptions, tradeoffs, and missing information rather than a single recommendation first.

coreConcepts.title: Keep The Human Owner Visible
coreConcepts.text: Decision authority should be explicit. AI is there to support judgment, not replace it.

workedExample.scenario: A manager must choose whether to delay a launch to improve onboarding.
workedExample.approach: They ask AI to lay out options, assumptions, affected stakeholders, and likely consequences.
workedExample.result: The choice becomes easier to discuss.
workedExample.verification: The manager checks the assumptions against actual constraints and team capacity before deciding.

doThisNow.task: Write a decision brief for one open decision you are facing.
steps: List the main constraints and assumptions.

commonMistakes: Asking AI to make the decision with no framing.
Treating a recommendation as a mandate.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to compare options, pressure-test plans, and prepare clearer stakeholder communication.
roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.
roleVariants.operator: Translate ambiguous work into checklists, workflows, and operating decisions.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Structure Options And Tradeoffs
lessons.subtitle: Good decisions compare meaningful choices against explicit criteria.
lessons.objective: Use AI to build option comparisons that are easier to evaluate and explain.
lessons.useWhen: Good decisions compare meaningful choices against explicit criteria.
lessons.whyItMatters: Many strategic discussions go nowhere because the options or evaluation criteria remain fuzzy.

coreConcepts.title: Name The Real Options
coreConcepts.text: A valid option set includes choices someone could actually approve and execute.

coreConcepts.title: Define The Criteria
coreConcepts.text: Cost, speed, risk, customer impact, and reversibility are common lenses.

coreConcepts.title: Show Tradeoffs Clearly
coreConcepts.text: A useful comparison makes the downside of each option visible, not just the upside.

workedExample.scenario: A program lead is comparing three rollout approaches.
workedExample.approach: They ask AI to build a criteria matrix and summarize tradeoffs in plain language.
workedExample.result: Stakeholders can compare the options more quickly.
workedExample.verification: The lead confirms that the criteria reflect what the decision-makers actually care about.

doThisNow.task: Create a tradeoff matrix for one live choice.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to compare options, pressure-test plans, and prepare clearer stakeholder communication.
roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.
roleVariants.operator: Translate ambiguous work into checklists, workflows, and operating decisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use AI As A Devil’s Advocate
lessons.subtitle: Pressure-test plans before other people do it for you.
lessons.objective: Use critique prompts to find weak assumptions, missing risks, and overlooked stakeholder concerns.
lessons.useWhen: Pressure-test plans before other people do it for you.
lessons.whyItMatters: A structured challenge pass reduces blind spots and improves your ability to defend a plan.

coreConcepts.title: Ask For Failure Modes
coreConcepts.text: Prompt for why the proposal might fail operationally, financially, or politically.

coreConcepts.title: Ask From Stakeholder Views
coreConcepts.text: Different stakeholders object for different reasons. Name those lenses explicitly.

coreConcepts.title: Turn Critique Into Revision
coreConcepts.text: A devil’s advocate step is useful only if it produces changes or better questions.

workedExample.scenario: A team wants to centralize a workflow quickly.
workedExample.approach: The lead asks AI to critique the plan from adoption, support, and executive visibility perspectives.
workedExample.result: Several realistic objections surface before the presentation.
workedExample.verification: The lead checks whether each objection is grounded in the real organization rather than generic caution.

doThisNow.task: Run a devil’s advocate pass on one current proposal.
steps: Revise the plan or message accordingly.

commonMistakes: Running critique with no follow-up revision.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Build Repeatable Decision Frameworks
lessons.subtitle: Recurring decisions deserve reusable structures.
lessons.objective: Turn repeated decisions into frameworks you can reuse and improve.
lessons.whyItMatters: A framework improves consistency and reduces the chance that each decision starts from scratch.

coreConcepts.title: Frameworks Fit Recurring Choices
coreConcepts.text: Vendor selection, staffing tradeoffs, rollout timing, and priority calls often repeat.

coreConcepts.title: Store Criteria And Questions
coreConcepts.text: The most reusable part of the framework is the evaluation logic.

coreConcepts.title: Keep It Lightweight
coreConcepts.text: A framework should guide judgment, not replace it with bureaucracy.

workedExample.scenario: A manager repeatedly chooses between feature requests with similar tradeoffs.
workedExample.approach: They build a simple framework with impact, effort, urgency, and dependency checks.
workedExample.result: The decision conversation becomes faster and more consistent.
workedExample.verification: They review the framework after a few uses to make sure it is still helping rather than oversimplifying.

doThisNow.task: Create a lightweight framework for one recurring decision type in your work.
steps: List what evidence you need each time.

commonMistakes: Forgetting to revisit the framework after use.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Create A Decision Support Toolkit
lessons.subtitle: Save the prompts, matrices, and critique patterns that improve strategic work.
lessons.objective: Build a reusable toolkit for option framing, tradeoff analysis, and critique.
lessons.useWhen: Save the prompts, matrices, and critique patterns that improve strategic work.
lessons.whyItMatters: Decision quality improves when the best questions and formats are easy to reach.

coreConcepts.title: Save The Decision Brief
coreConcepts.text: A clear framing format helps every later prompt perform better.

coreConcepts.title: Save The Comparison Template
coreConcepts.text: Matrices and summary formats make stakeholder discussions clearer.

coreConcepts.title: Save The Critique Pass
coreConcepts.text: Every major decision should have a reusable challenge step.

workedExample.scenario: A strategy lead makes frequent tradeoff calls but improvises the AI prompts each time.
workedExample.approach: They create a toolkit with a decision brief, a comparison matrix, and a critique prompt.
workedExample.result: Their process becomes easier to repeat and teach.
workedExample.verification: They test the toolkit on a second decision to ensure the prompts are not overfit to one case.

doThisNow.task: Package one decision workflow into a reusable toolkit entry.

commonMistakes: Assuming one strong prompt covers every decision type.

outcomes: Use AI to challenge assumptions before decisions are made.
nextModuleBridge: Next, apply that same structured thinking to vendors, contracts, and stakeholder work.
